skill.name: ad-creative
skill.description: When the user wants to generate, iterate, or scale ad creative — headlines, descriptions, primary text, or full ad variations — for any paid advertising platform. Also use when the user mentions 'ad copy variations,' 'ad creative,' 'generate headlines,' 'RSA headlines,' 'bulk ad copy,' 'ad iterations,' 'creative testing,' 'ad performance optimization,' 'write me some ads,' 'Facebook ad copy,' 'Google ad headlines,' 'LinkedIn ad text,' or 'I need more ad variations.' Use this whenever someone needs to produce ad copy at scale or iterate on existing ads. For campaign strategy and targeting, see paid-ads. For landing page copy, see copywriting.
skill.summary: Generate and iterate high-performing ad creative across Google, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok using angle-based frameworks and performance data analysis.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: All 158 static findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are misidentified terms: RSA means Responsive Search Ads (Google Ads format), not encryption. Other flags stem from markdown documentation containing API examples, reference URLs, and relative file paths. No executable code or malicious patterns exist in this instructional skill.

content.user_title: Generate high-performing ad creative at scale
content.value_statement: Struggling to produce enough ad variations for testing? This skill generates platform-perfect ad copy using proven angle frameworks and performance data to drive clicks and conversions.
actual_capabilities: Generate platform-compliant ad copy for Google RSAs, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok with correct character limits
Create angle-based ad variations (pain point, social proof, curiosity, urgency, contrarian)
Analyze performance data (CTR, CPC, conversion rate) to identify winning creative patterns
Iterate on existing ads by doubling down on winning themes and retiring underperformers
Generate bulk ad variations (10-100+) organized for direct CSV upload to ad platforms
Suggest visual and creative direction for each ad angle
limitations: Does not manage ad campaigns or budgets directly — requires separate paid-ads skill for strategy
Cannot access ad platform APIs directly — user must provide performance data
Does not generate actual images or videos — provides prompts and direction for generative AI tools
Platform character limits may change — verify against current specs before publishing

use_cases.title: B2B SaaS launching Google Ads campaign
use_cases.description: A marketing manager needs 15 headlines and 4 descriptions for a new CRM product targeting 'best CRM for small business' keyword. The skill generates RSA-compliant copy with keyword integration, benefit statements, and CTAs — all under 30/90 character limits.

use_cases.title: E-commerce brand iterating Meta ads
use_cases.description: A DTC brand provides performance data showing pain point angles have 2.1% CTR but social proof has 6.2% conversion. The skill analyzes winners, recommends pausing underperformers, and generates new variations combining pain point hooks with social proof elements.

use_cases.title: Enterprise security company creating LinkedIn ads
use_cases.description: A security vendor targets CISOs with compliance-focused messaging. The skill generates formal, risk-reduction language across intro text (150 chars), headline (70 chars), and description (100 chars) — matching the professional tone and character constraints for LinkedIn.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Google Ads from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Google Responsive Search Ads for [product]. Target keyword: '[keyword]'. Audience: [audience description]. Value proposition: [core benefit]. Include 15 headlines (30 chars max) and 4 descriptions (90 chars max). Use angle-based approach: pain point, benefit, social proof, curiosity, and CTA angles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting fresh with a new product campaign

prompt_templates.title: Generate Meta ads with visual direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad creative for [product/service]. Audience: [target audience]. Pain points: [list 2-3]. Generate 5 angles with primary text (125 chars), headline (40 chars), and description (30 chars). For each angle, suggest image/visual direction that would work well.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating social media ads with creative guidance

prompt_templates.title: Iterate ads from performance data
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this ad performance and generate new variations:

Ad A: [copy] — CTR: [x]%, Conv: [y]%
Ad B: [copy] — CTR: [x]%, Conv: [y]%
Ad C: [copy] — CTR: [x]%, Conv: [y]%

Identify winning patterns, recommend what to pause, and generate 10 new variations that build on what's working.
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving existing ads based on performance metrics

prompt_templates.title: Multi-platform batch generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate ad creative for a multi-platform campaign (Google, Meta, TikTok) for [product]. Target audience: [audience]. Generate platform-specific variations respecting each platform's character limits. Organize output by platform. For TikTok, include video concept suggestions. Produce 20+ total variations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Scaling creative production across multiple channels

output_examples.input: Generate Google RSA headlines for a project management tool targeting 'agile project management software'
Iterate these Meta ads: Ad A (pain: Stop wasting hours on manual reports) CTR 2.3%, Conv 3.1%. Ad B (proof: 10,000 teams automate with us) CTR 1.8%, Conv 5.4%.
output: Analysis: Ad A wins on CTR (pain resonates), Ad B wins on conversion (social proof builds trust). Recommendation: Combine pain + proof in new variations. Retire generic feature angles.
New Variation 1: 'Stop building reports by hand. Join 10,000+ teams who automate with us. Start free.'
New Variation 2: 'Manual reporting eating your week? See why 10K teams switched to automation.'

best_practices: Always verify character counts before delivering — platforms truncate without warning
Generate headlines that make sense independently — RSA headlines combine randomly
Include at least one CTA headline and one keyword-focused headline per ad set
Base iteration decisions on performance data, not gut feelings — 1,000+ impressions minimum
anti_patterns: Writing headlines that only work together — each must stand alone in RSAs
Generating variations that sound identical — vary angles, not just synonyms
Using generic descriptions like 'Learn more' — add proof points or handle objections
Retiring creative too early — allow sufficient impressions before judging performance

faq.question: What's the difference between this skill and paid-ads?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on generating ad creative (headlines, descriptions, primary text). Use paid-ads for campaign strategy, platform selection, budget allocation, targeting, and bidding strategies.

faq.question: Can this skill generate ads for platforms not listed?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the platform's character limits and format requirements, and the skill will generate compliant creative. Core support covers Google, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter/X.

faq.question: How many ad variations should I generate for testing?
faq.answer: Start with 10-15 headlines and 4-5 descriptions per ad set. For RSAs, Google needs 10+ headlines and 4+ descriptions to optimize. Test 3-5 distinct angles with 3-5 variations each.

faq.question: Can this skill pull performance data from ad platforms directly?
faq.answer: No. You must provide performance data (CSV paste, screenshots, or CLI tool output). Use the tools registry CLIs to pull data, then feed it to this skill for analysis and iteration.

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual ad images or videos?
faq.answer: No. It provides creative direction and prompts for image/video generation. Use the referenced generative-tools guide for AI image/video tools like Nano Banana Pro, Flux, Veo, and Kling.

faq.question: How often should I iterate on ad creative?
faq.answer: Allow 1,000+ impressions and 7-14 days before judging performance. Iterate weekly if spending $1k+/week, biweekly for smaller budgets. Always be testing 1-2 new angles against control winners.
